lines.speaker: Wizard Persten
lines.text: Hello!

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Hey there.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Who are you?

lines.speaker: Wizard Persten
lines.text: What? You haven't heard of me? The great Mia Persten of the Order of Wizards? The leading researcher in teleportation magic? The creator of the Home Teleport spell?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Oh, I'm sorry. I didn't mean to offend you. I'm [player name].

lines.speaker: Wizard Persten
lines.text: No offence taken! My research tends to take me far from the Wizards' Tower. I've been all across Gielinor and even to some other planes of existence!

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Other planes of existence? Sounds exciting!

lines.speaker: Wizard Persten
lines.text: Exciting and terrifying! Brilliant, right?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: So what are you doing in Al Kharid?

lines.speaker: Wizard Persten
lines.text: Should I not be in Al Kharid?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Er... good question. I guess I just haven't seen a wizard around here before.

lines.speaker: Wizard Persten
lines.text: Well, maybe you should start expecting the unexpected! That way you can spend less time worrying about why I'm here, and more time asking life's real questions.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: What's a wizard doing in Al Kharid?

lines.speaker: Wizard Persten
lines.text: Why shouldn't a wizard be in Al Kharid?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Er... good question. I guess I just haven't seen a wizard around here before.

lines.speaker: Wizard Persten
lines.text: Well for a start, I'm not just any wizard. The name's Mia Persten. I'm the leading researcher in teleportation magic for the Order of Wizards.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Pleased to meet you. I'm [player name].

lines.speaker: Wizard Persten
lines.text: Now as for your question, maybe you should start expecting the unexpected! That way you can spend less time worrying about why I'm here, and more time asking life's real questions.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Right... and what kind of questions are you referring to?

lines.speaker: Wizard Persten
lines.text: Well, that's the thing. It's different for everyone! Why do trees grow from stumps substantially faster than from seeds? Where do meteorites come from? Why do we have to hold our arms out to teleport successfully?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I don't know. What are the answers?

lines.speaker: Wizard Persten
lines.text: I don't know either!

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: So you're in Al Kharid to work out how trees grow?

lines.speaker: Wizard Persten
lines.text: No, silly! I came here to buy something.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Something?

lines.speaker: Wizard Persten
lines.text: That's right! Something! Tell me, what do you know of runecrafting?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Not a lot really.

lines.speaker: Wizard Persten
lines.text: A shame. Unfortunately, this something requires some understanding in that area.

lines.speaker: Wizard Persten
lines.text: That's right! Something! Tell me, what do you know of the Abyss?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Not a lot really.

lines.speaker: Wizard Persten
lines.text: A shame. Unfortunately, this something requires somewhat of an abyssal understanding.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: The Abyss? I know all about the Abyss.

lines.speaker: Wizard Persten
lines.text: Really? It's quite unusual for an adventurer like yourself to have knowledge in that area.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Well I recently helped a group of Zamorakian wizards gain access to the Rune Essence Mine. In return, they gave me access to the Abyss.

lines.speaker: Wizard Persten
lines.text: Zamorakian wizards? You do realise that the Z.M.I. are enemies to the Order of Wizards, right?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Maybe I shouldn't have mentioned that...

lines.speaker: Wizard Persten
lines.text: Oh, don't worry about it. The Order of Wizards may be a Saradominist group, but I've never personally had much time for gods. I prefer knowledge to religion.

lines.speaker: Wizard Persten
lines.text: Still, it's maybe best you don't tell anyone else. I can't imagine my fellow wizards will be happy to know that you gave away details on their secret mine.

lines.speaker: Wizard Persten
lines.text: Enough about them though. You've just shared quite the revelation with me! Is it really true that the Z.M.I. have found a way to access the Abyss?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: That's right.

lines.speaker: Wizard Persten
lines.text: Well in that case, I think you might be the perfect person to aid me in a little task.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: A little task? This sounds like the time someone asked me for one small favour. I'm not sure if I'm ready for that again.

lines.speaker: Wizard Persten
lines.text: Oh, don't be silly! This will be fun!

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Alright, what kind of task is it?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: What kind of task?

lines.speaker: Wizard Persten
lines.text: Well I can't go telling you that until you agree, can I? Wouldn't want you sharing my secrets.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: You're not giving me much to go on here.

lines.speaker: Wizard Persten
lines.text: There'll be quite the reward for you at the end!

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I'd rather not get involved.

lines.speaker: Wizard Persten
lines.text: That's a shame. Well, you just let me know if you reconsider.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: See you around.

sections.title: Talking to Wizard Persten after declining to start the quest

lines.speaker: Wizard Persten
lines.text: Hello again! Have you changed your mind about helping me with that little task?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Alright, what do you need from me?

lines.speaker: Wizard Persten
lines.text: You'll help? Great! Well first, a question for you: you say the Z.M.I. have gained access to the Abyss? By any chance, are they using it to gain quick access to the runic altars for runecrafting purposes?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Yes, but how did you...

lines.speaker: Wizard Persten
lines.text: I knew it! I've been wondering for some time how they've been creating so many runes. I'd never considered that they'd somehow gained access to the Abyss, but I now realise it was the obvious explanation.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: So is that what you're after? You want access to the Abyss as well?

lines.speaker: Wizard Persten
lines.text: Well, yes, but actually, no. It seems we've jumped ahead a bit. Let's go back to why I'm here.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: The 'something' you bought?

lines.speaker: Wizard Persten
lines.text: The 'something' I bought! Here, take a look.

lines.speaker: Wizard Persten
lines.text: A foolish archaeologist by the name of Asgarnia Smith recently found this washed up near the Ruins of Uzer. Despite his apparent archaeological credentials, he dismissed it as junk.

lines.speaker: Wizard Persten
lines.text: Luckily, I was able to buy it from him before he disposed of it. Can you believe he thought the 100 coins I paid was too much? If only he knew the true value of something like this...

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Why is it so valuable?

lines.speaker: Wizard Persten
lines.text: You see that eye on it? That is the symbol of both the Order of Wizards and our home, the Wizards' Tower. Thing is, that's only the start.

lines.speaker: Wizard Persten
lines.text: From what I can tell, this amulet is thousands of years old, but our order was only formed at the dawn of the Fifth Age. That wasn't even two hundred years ago!

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: But how...

lines.speaker: Wizard Persten
lines.text: Hang on! I'm not done yet. I also inspected the amulet for magical energies and found something fascinating! I recognised two distinct magical signatures.

lines.speaker: Wizard Persten
lines.text: The first is the same magic found in rune essence. That's already pretty exciting, but it's the second that will blow your mind!

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: What was it?

lines.speaker: Wizard Persten
lines.text: I'm getting to that! My, you do like interrupting, don't you? Don't worry, I understand. I get quite excited about these things as well!

lines.speaker: Wizard Persten
lines.text: Anyway, here's the big reveal for you: the second signature was energy from the Abyss!

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: So you have an amulet with the symbol of your order on it, but it predates your order by thousands of years?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: It also turned up miles from where your order is based, and is filled with energy from a dimension that until recently, no one had even been to?

lines.speaker: Wizard Persten
lines.text: Now you see why this is so exciting!

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: So what do you need from me?

lines.speaker: Wizard Persten
lines.text: I need to find out where this amulet came from. It should be possible to trace it using the abyssal energy it still contains.

lines.speaker: Wizard Persten
lines.text: This is where you ask 'What's the catch?'

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: *sigh* What's the catch?

lines.speaker: Wizard Persten
lines.text: I'm glad you asked, [player name]! Unfortunately, the spell needed to trace the amulet will only work properly from inside the Abyss.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Oh I see where this is going. You don't have access to the Abyss, but I do.

lines.speaker: Wizard Persten
lines.text: Precisely! While I don't care about the religious squabbling between the Z.M.I. and my order, I still can't see them wanting to work with me any time soon.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: So I need to take this amulet to the Abyss and work out where it came from?

lines.speaker: Wizard Persten
lines.text: Correct! I imagine you'll need the help of your Z.M.I. friends.

lines.speaker: Wizard Persten
lines.text: Just maybe don't tell them who gave it to you.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I'll visit the Mage of Zamorak in Varrock. He should be able to help me.

lines.speaker: Wizard Persten
lines.text: Return to me once you are done. Oh, and don't forget the amulet!

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I'd rather not get involved.

lines.speaker: Wizard Persten
lines.text: That's a shame. Well, you just let me know if you reconsider.

sections.title: Talking to Wizard Persten again before getting the amulet

lines.speaker: Wizard Persten
lines.text: Hello again! Here, take this amulet to your Z.M.I. friend in Varrock. Hopefully he can tell us where it came from.

lines.speaker: Wizard Persten
lines.text: Hello again! Any luck with that amulet?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Sorry, but I lost it.

lines.speaker: Wizard Persten
lines.text: You lost a one of a kind ancient artefact?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Yes...

lines.speaker: Wizard Persten
lines.text: Well, good job I found it again. Here you go. Take it to your Z.M.I. friend in Varrock. Hopefully he can tell us where it came from.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Not yet.

lines.speaker: Wizard Persten
lines.text: You should take it to your Z.M.I. friend in Varrock. Hopefully he can tell us where it came from.

sections.title: Talking to the Mage of Zamorak
Talking to the Mage of Zamorak again

lines.speaker: Mage of Zamorak
lines.text: Zamorak: Ah, you again. What do you want?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Could you help me with that amulet now?

lines.speaker: Mage of Zamorak
lines.text: Do you have a bucket of water and a strong cup of tea?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Not yet.

lines.speaker: Mage of Zamorak
lines.text: I will not help you until you bring them to me. The tea seller just north east of here can give you a strong cup of tea. Just tell him Herbert sent you.

lines.speaker: Mage of Zamorak
lines.text: Ah, you again. What do you want?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I need your help with an amulet.

lines.speaker: Mage of Zamorak
lines.text: An amulet? What amulet?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Oh, I don't actually have it with me. I'll be back.

lines.speaker: Mage of Zamorak
lines.text: Right...

lines.speaker: Mage of Zamorak
lines.text: That eye on it... That is the symbol of the Order of Wizards. What's all this about?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: The amulet is giving off abyssal energy. I was hoping you could help me work out where it came from.

lines.speaker: Mage of Zamorak
lines.text: Why should I?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Well I did help you gain access to the Rune Essence Mine.

lines.speaker: Mage of Zamorak
lines.text: And we gave you knowledge on the Abyss in return. We owe you no further favours.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Are you not even slightly curious about how an amulet with the symbol of the Order of Wizards on it is giving off abyssal energy? After all, they don't have access to the Abyss.

lines.speaker: Mage of Zamorak
lines.text: I hope you're not about to tell me that you shared our secrets with the Saradominists?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Of course not.

lines.speaker: Mage of Zamorak
lines.text: Then where did you get the amulet?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I... bought it from an archaeologist.

lines.speaker: Mage of Zamorak
lines.text: There's something off about all of this. Show it to me again.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: What was that?

lines.speaker: Mage of Zamorak
lines.text: Just a spell to tell me more about it. It seems this amulet is very unusual.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: So you'll help?

lines.speaker: Mage of Zamorak
lines.text: Very well, but first you must do something for me. Bring me a bucket of water and a strong cup of tea.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: A strong cup of tea?

lines.speaker: Mage of Zamorak
lines.text: That's right. The tea seller just north east of here can give you one. He owes me, in fact, so just tell him Herbert sent you.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Wait... Your name is Herbert?

lines.speaker: Mage of Zamorak
lines.text: Is that a problem?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I guess not. I just never had you down as a Herbert.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I'll be back soon.

sections.title: Talking to the Tea Seller again

lines.speaker: Tea Seller
lines.text: Greetings! Are you in need of refreshment?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Could I have a strong cup of tea?

lines.speaker: Tea Seller
lines.text: For Herbert? I already gave you one for him.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Sorry, but I need another.

lines.speaker: Tea Seller
lines.text: Hmm... alright, but I can't keep making these for free you know.

lines.speaker: Tea Seller
lines.text: I hope Herbert enjoys it.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Ah yes, how silly of me.

lines.speaker: Tea Seller
lines.text: Greetings! Are you in need of refreshment?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Could I have a strong cup of tea?

lines.speaker: Tea Seller
lines.text: A strong cup of tea? What do you mean?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Herbert sent me for one.

lines.speaker: Tea Seller
lines.text: Ah, well why didn't you say that? Between you and me, Herbert's strong cups of tea are actually just my regular cups of tea, but if it makes him happy...

lines.speaker: Tea Seller
lines.text: I hope Herbert enjoys it.

sections.title: Talking to the Mage of Zamorak after getting the tea

lines.speaker: Mage of Zamorak
lines.text: Ah, you again. What do you want?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Could you help me with that amulet now?

lines.speaker: Mage of Zamorak
lines.text: Do you have a bucket of water and a strong cup of tea?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Not yet.

lines.speaker: Mage of Zamorak
lines.text: I will not help you until you bring them to me.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Here you go.

lines.speaker: Mage of Zamorak
lines.text: Aaah, nothing like a nice cuppa tea!

lines.speaker: Mage of Zamorak
lines.text: Right, a deal is a deal, but the spell to determine the origin of that amulet will only work from the Abyss, so you'll need to have the initiate there perform it for you.

lines.speaker: Mage of Zamorak
lines.text: Meet me in the Wilderness when you're ready to be teleported there.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Can't you just teleport me from here?

lines.speaker: Mage of Zamorak
lines.text: From the middle of Varrock? What if someone is watching?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: If someone was watching, they'd know you have a secret Chaos Temple here in Varrock. I reckon you're pretty safe.

lines.speaker: Mage of Zamorak
lines.text: Fine, but don't expect me to make a habit of this. I will offer you a single teleport from here directly to the initiate in the Abyss. Make sure you are definitely ready.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Hang on... what was the bucket of water for?

lines.speaker: Mage of Zamorak
lines.text: Ah, that. While time passes slower in the Abyss, the initiate maintaining the rift has still been there for quite a while. He needs a good bath.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: So you just want me to throw it over him?

lines.speaker: Mage of Zamorak
lines.text: Exactly. Now, are you ready to be teleported? Don't forget, this is a one-time offer. In future, you'll need to meet me in the Wilderness to access the Abyss.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I should probably get the amulet first.

lines.speaker: Mage of Zamorak
lines.text: That would be wise.

lines.speaker: Mage of Zamorak
lines.text: Veniens! Sallakar! Rinnesset!

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I'm not ready yet.

lines.speaker: Mage of Zamorak
lines.text: Well then return to me when you are, but try not to waste too much of my time.

sections.title: Talking to the Mage of Zamorak again after he drinks the tea
Talking to the Mage of Zamorak again after using the one-time teleport

lines.speaker: Mage of Zamorak

lines.speaker: Mage of Zamorak

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Alright.

lines.text: That would be wise.

lines.speaker: Mage of Zamorak

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Hello there.

lines.speaker: Dark Mage
lines.text: Quiet! You must not break my concentration!

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I need your help with an amulet.

lines.speaker: Dark Mage
lines.text: Amulet?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Yes, but I don't seem to have it with me. I'll be back soon.

lines.speaker: Dark Mage
lines.text: What? Are you trying to waste my time?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Yes, but I also need to go and get a bucket of water.

lines.speaker: Dark Mage
lines.text: What? Are you trying to waste my time?

lines.speaker: Dark Mage
lines.text: Oh, hang on. Someone is trying to contact me...

lines.speaker: Dark Mage
lines.text: What do you want? Can't you see I'm busy?

lines.speaker: Dark Mage
lines.text: Fine. A simple transfiguration spell should resolve things for you.

lines.speaker: Dark Mage
lines.text: Now can you leave me alone? I can't keep affording these distractions!

lines.speaker: Dark Mage
lines.text: What did you want? Something about an amulet?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Yes, but first...

lines.speaker: Dark Mage
lines.text: What in the actual...

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Sorry, but Herbert said you needed a wash.

lines.speaker: Dark Mage
lines.text: Are you serious? What if I'd lost concentration and let this rift collapse? Do you like having a brain that isn't made of mush? Because mush is all that would have been left!

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I like my brain how it is, thanks. Now, could you help me?

lines.speaker: Dark Mage
lines.text: Why should I? I'm soaked thanks to you!

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Herbert said you would.

lines.speaker: Dark Mage
lines.text: Of course he did! Alright then, what do you want?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: This amulet is giving off abyssal energy. I need you to cast a spell to tell me where it came from.

lines.speaker: Dark Mage
lines.text: Let's have a look then.

lines.speaker: Dark Mage
lines.text: Right, just give me a moment.

lines.speaker: Dark Mage
lines.text: Okay, this spell will allow me to triangulate the origin of the amulet. To make things easier, I'm using this rift to compare energy from that origin with energy from the runic altars.

lines.speaker: Dark Mage
lines.text: Of course, thanks to the ways time and space work in this place, the information won't necessarily come in the right order.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Meaning?

lines.speaker: Dark Mage
lines.text: Meaning I'll need you to help, you fool! You see that energy that's just emerged from the rift? Work out which order it goes in!

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Right...
