sections.title: Talking to the Dark Mage after the energy spawns

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Hello there.

lines.speaker: Dark Mage
lines.text: Quiet! You must not break my concentration!

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: What do I need to do with this energy?

lines.speaker: Dark Mage
lines.text: Work out which order it goes in!

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Right...

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Hello there.

lines.speaker: Dark Mage
lines.text: Quiet! You must not break my concentration!

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: About that incantation...

lines.speaker: Dark Mage
lines.text: What about it?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I lost it.

lines.speaker: Dark Mage
lines.text: Oh the things I would do to you if I wasn't maintaining this rift! Here, take this you fool!

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Er... Nothing.

lines.speaker: Dark Mage
lines.text: Nothing! Would you leave me alone!

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I've worked out which order the energy goes in.

lines.speaker: Dark Mage
lines.text: Yes, I can see that. Now, hang on.

lines.speaker: Dark Mage
lines.text: I've noted the details on this scroll. A competent enough wizard should be able to use it to cast a teleportation spell to take you right there.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Can you tell me anything about the place?

lines.speaker: Dark Mage
lines.text: With all the time I've spent here, I'd like to consider myself an expert on the Abyss, but there's something very strange about the place this amulet came from.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Strange how?

lines.speaker: Dark Mage
lines.text: Well if I knew that, it wouldn't be strange, would it? Now, if you're done bothering me, I need to stay focused on this rift!

sections.title: Talking to the Mage of Zamorak after getting the incantation

lines.speaker: Mage of Zamorak
lines.text: Ah, you again. What do you want?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Could you help me with that amulet now?

lines.speaker: Mage of Zamorak
lines.text: The spell to determine the origin of that amulet will only work from the Abyss, so you'll need to have the initiate there perform it for you.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Oh, he already did that.

lines.speaker: Mage of Zamorak
lines.text: Then why are you talking to me? You have what you need.

lines.speaker: Wizard Persten
lines.text: Hello again! Any luck with that amulet?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Yes! I have an incantation that could lead us to wherever it came from.

lines.speaker: Wizard Persten
lines.text: Brilliant! Let's have a look.

lines.speaker: Wizard Persten
lines.text: Oh, fascinating! Hmm... But that would mean...

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: That would mean what?

lines.speaker: Wizard Persten
lines.text: Shhh! I'm thinking.

lines.speaker: Wizard Persten
lines.text: Yes... Two... no three more! Silly me! Counted myself didn't I.

lines.speaker: Wizard Persten
lines.text: Well it should be fine. I can't see them saying no. After all, it's their symbol as well...

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: What are you on about?

lines.speaker: Wizard Persten
lines.text: Oh yes, you're here! Brilliant! You'll be pleased to know that I've worked out what we must do next.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: And that is?

lines.speaker: Wizard Persten
lines.text: Patience, young adventurer. Before we get to that, let me share with you my theory on where this amulet came from.

lines.speaker: Wizard Persten
lines.text: While you were gone, I was thinking about why the amulet would have magical energy from both rune essence and the Abyss. All of a sudden, it hit me!

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Ouch?

lines.speaker: Wizard Persten
lines.text: Quite the opposite! The source of this amulet must be an ancient teleportation matrix to the runic altars, just like the one the Z.M.I. have in the Abyss!

lines.speaker: Wizard Persten
lines.text: This is good news, in case you were wondering! Access to a teleportation matrix like that will be life changing when it comes to my work!

lines.speaker: Wizard Persten
lines.text: Of course, the rest of my order will be very happy as well. It'll completely remove the advantage the Z.M.I. currently have. Now there's a balance that Guthix himself would love!

lines.speaker: Wizard Persten
lines.text: Ah, but this talk of the Z.M.I. has reminded me of something. Could I have that amulet back?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Here you go.

lines.speaker: Wizard Persten
lines.text: Ah, but this talk of the Z.M.I. has reminded me of something. You lost that amulet didn't you?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Maybe...

lines.speaker: Wizard Persten
lines.text: Don't worry, I found it for you. I'll keep hold of it for now.

lines.speaker: Wizard Persten
lines.text: Now, just give me a moment...

lines.speaker: Wizard Persten
lines.text: Ah, yes. Well, that's not surprising.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: What is it?

lines.speaker: Wizard Persten
lines.text: Your Z.M.I. friends cast a transmission spell on the amulet. Had we taken it with us, it would have transmitted all sorts of information back to them about the origin.

lines.speaker: Wizard Persten
lines.text: It's not a problem. I suspected they might do something like this. The good news is that this means they have no intention of using that incantation themselves.

lines.speaker: Wizard Persten
lines.text: Again, as expected. They prefer to lurk in the shadows. To be honest, I totally understand. I've been known to do the same from time to time.

lines.speaker: Wizard Persten
lines.text: Anyway, I've modified their spell to send false information to them. They'll be none the wiser.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I thought you didn't care about religion? Why don't you want the Z.M.I. getting that information?

lines.speaker: Wizard Persten
lines.text: Oh, my friend, you misunderstand. I have no issues with the Z.M.I. having the information, but I don't want them getting it before I've even had a chance to determine what it is!

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I see. So what now?

lines.speaker: Wizard Persten
lines.text: Yes, back to business! The incantation is quite complex, so we will need the help of three other wizards to perform it. I'm afraid I'm not including you in that, although you are very talented!

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Thanks, I think... so where do we get these other wizards?

lines.speaker: Wizard Persten
lines.text: From the Wizards' Tower of course! My fellow wizards won't be able to resist getting their hands on a teleportation matrix, especially one that may be connected to the history of our order!

lines.speaker: Wizard Persten
lines.text: Now, you head to the Wizards' Tower and show that incantation to Sedridor. I have a couple of things to prepare first, but I will join you there soon.

lines.speaker: Wizard Persten
lines.text: Oh, and whatever you do, don't mention the Z.M.I. while you're there.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Alright, I'll see you soon.

lines.speaker: Wizard Persten
lines.text: Hey, if you want, I can teleport you to the Wizards' Tower. Consider it a one-time gift from me.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I think I'll walk.

lines.speaker: Wizard Persten
lines.text: Well let me know if you change your mind.

sections.title: Talking to Wizard Persten again before using the one-time teleport

lines.speaker: Wizard Persten
lines.text: Hello again! Did you take that incantation to Sedridor?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I may have lost it.

lines.speaker: Wizard Persten
lines.text: No matter, I can give you a copy of mine. Try not to lose it this time!

lines.speaker: Wizard Persten
lines.text: Now quickly! Time is of the essence! If you want, I can teleport you to the Wizards' Tower. Consider it a one-time gift from me.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I think I'll walk.

lines.speaker: Wizard Persten
lines.text: Well let me know if you change your mind.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Not yet.

lines.speaker: Wizard Persten
lines.text: Well hurry up! If you want, I can teleport you to the Wizards' Tower. Consider it a one-time gift from me.

sections.title: Talking to Wizard Persten after using the one-time teleport

lines.speaker: Wizard Persten
lines.text: Hello again! Did you take that incantation to Sedridor?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I may have lost it.

lines.speaker: Wizard Persten
lines.text: No matter, I can give you a copy of mine. Try not to lose it this time!

lines.speaker: Wizard Persten
lines.text: Now quickly! Time is of the essence!

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Not yet.

lines.speaker: Wizard Persten
lines.text: Well hurry up! This could be the biggest discovery of our lifetimes!

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Hello there.

lines.speaker: Archmage Sedridor
lines.text: Hello again, [player name]. What can I do for you?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Can you help me with that incantation?

lines.speaker: Archmage Sedridor
lines.text: I haven't finished my analysis of it yet. While you wait, I suggest you speak with Traiborn. He has three new apprentices who could help you, assuming I agree to let things go ahead.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Hello there.

lines.speaker: Archmage Sedridor
lines.text: Hello again, [player name]. What can I do for you?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I need your help with an incantation.

lines.speaker: Archmage Sedridor
lines.text: An incantation, hmm? What incantation?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: A teleport that could potentially take us to an ancient teleportation matrix. Persten reckons you might be able to use it to gain easier access to the runic altars.

lines.speaker: Archmage Sedridor
lines.text: Wait... Persten sent you?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: That's right. Oh, I should also mention that this teleportation matrix seems to have some connection to your order, despite predating it by thousands of years.

lines.speaker: Archmage Sedridor
lines.text: So Persten disappears for all this time, and then shows up again with knowledge on an ancient teleportation matrix? I suppose I shouldn't be surprised. It's probably not the strangest thing she's done.

lines.speaker: Archmage Sedridor
lines.text: Anyway, what do you need my help with exactly?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Persten needs three other wizards to be able to perform the incantation.

lines.speaker: Archmage Sedridor
lines.text: Hmm... can I see it?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Oh, I don't have it with me.

lines.speaker: Archmage Sedridor
lines.text: I'd very much like to see it. Come back to me when you have it.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Sure.

lines.speaker: Archmage Sedridor
lines.text: Hmm... this scroll is very interesting. Did you get it from a Zamorakian wizard, perhaps? Don't lie to me, [player name].

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Well since you asked... I... well...

lines.speaker: Archmage Sedridor
lines.text: It's clear from your response that my suspicions are correct. This incantation is written on very thin flesh rather than parchment, a horrific practice only used by Zamorakians.

lines.speaker: Archmage Sedridor
lines.text: Need I remind you that it was the Zamorakians who burnt down the first Wizards' Tower?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Er...

lines.speaker: Archmage Sedridor
lines.text: [player name], I'm going to be frank with you. Persten hasn't been seen here for almost a year. My last meeting with her was to discuss changing her line of study due to her lack of results.

lines.speaker: Archmage Sedridor
lines.text: She was given one more year to produce something of note. Now, with time almost up, she sends you here with Zamorakian incantations and odd claims about an ancient teleportation matrix.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: But what if it's true? That teleportation matrix could change everything!

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Okay, I admit we went to some Zamorakians for help with the incantation, but they've had no other involvement. Persten has even made sure they won't get anything beneficial out of this.

lines.speaker: Archmage Sedridor
lines.text: Hmm... You have been a great help to us in the past. For that, I will consider it. First though, I will do a thorough arcane analysis of this incantation to determine if it is safe to cast.

lines.speaker: Archmage Sedridor
lines.text: Now, as for your three wizards, I can't be wasting the others' time with this sort of thing, especially when I haven't even agreed to allow it yet.

lines.speaker: Archmage Sedridor
lines.text: However, Traiborn has three new apprentices who could help. This might be a good learning opportunity for them. Speak to him while I inspect this incantation.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Okay, thanks.

sections.title: Talking to the apprentices before speaking to Traiborn

lines.speaker: Apprentice Cordelia
lines.text: Thingummywut. Thin... gummy... what?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: What are you talking about?

lines.speaker: Apprentice Cordelia
lines.text: I'm a little busy trying to solve that mad wizard's puzzle. If you have any questions, ask him instead.

lines.speaker: Apprentice Felix
lines.text: So, if that's six and this is eight, then if we multiply... No, divide? No, that's not it either.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: What are you talking about?

lines.speaker: Apprentice Felix
lines.text: Oh, didn't see you there! I'm a little busy trying to solve a puzzle set by the great Traiborn. If you have any questions, could you ask him instead?

lines.speaker: Apprentice Tamara
lines.text: Air and earth? What about water and fire? Is this an elemental problem, or are they completely irrelevant?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: What are you talking about?

lines.speaker: Apprentice Tamara
lines.text: Oh, didn't see you there. I'm a little busy trying to solve the puzzle Traiborn gave us. If you have any questions, you should ask him instead.

lines.speaker: Wizard Traiborn
lines.text: Ello young thingummywut.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I think I know what a thingummywut is!

lines.speaker: Wizard Traiborn
lines.text: Oh? Do tell.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: A slayer creature!

lines.speaker: Wizard Traiborn
lines.text: A slayer creature? That doesn't make any sense at all! Besides, not even the slayer masters know how to kill a thingummywut!

lines.speaker: Wizard Traiborn
lines.text: Perhaps my apprentices could teach you a thing or three. You should talk to them.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: A great wizard, lost to history!

lines.speaker: Wizard Traiborn
lines.text: Lost to history? That doesn't make any sense at all! Besides, a thingummywut is not lost to history. It's very well documented inside my head!

lines.speaker: Wizard Traiborn
lines.text: Perhaps my apprentices could teach you a thing or three. You should talk to them.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: You!

lines.speaker: Wizard Traiborn
lines.text: Me? That doesn't make any sense at all! If I were a thingummywut, I wouldn't be stood here teaching!

lines.speaker: Wizard Traiborn
lines.text: Perhaps my apprentices could teach you a thing or three. You should talk to them.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I don't know.

lines.speaker: Wizard Traiborn
lines.text: Oh dear, how embarrassing. Perhaps my apprentices could teach you a thing or three. You should talk to them.

lines.speaker: Wizard Traiborn
lines.text: Ello young thingummywut.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I need your apprentices to help with an incantation.

lines.speaker: Wizard Traiborn
lines.text: Right now? But we're having a party!

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: A party?

lines.speaker: Wizard Traiborn
lines.text: Party? What party?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: You said you were having a party?

lines.speaker: Wizard Traiborn
lines.text: Well I suppose it is a party of sorts. A study party! Would you like to join in? You look like you could use one!

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I'll pass... Anyway, Sedridor said your apprentices could help me with an incantation.

lines.speaker: Wizard Traiborn
lines.text: What kind of incantation?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: One that could lead us to an ancient teleportation matrix.

lines.speaker: Wizard Traiborn
lines.text: Teleportation matrix? Doesn't sound like the most comfortable place for a party... I bet it's full of thingummywuts as well!

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I'm not suggesting we move the party there. I'm suggesting that we cancel the party and discover the secrets of this teleportation matrix instead.

lines.speaker: Wizard Traiborn
lines.text: Cancel the party? You're certainly a party pooper!

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Alright, how about instead of cancelling the party, we finish it with a bang? What's currently on the agenda?

lines.speaker: Wizard Traiborn
lines.text: Finish it with a bang? Sedridor won't be happy if we burn the place down again, will he? Still, I like it!

lines.speaker: Wizard Traiborn
lines.text: I've currently tasked each of the apprentices with solving a problem. If they all solve it, we can celebrate together!

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Sounds good. So, what's the problem?

lines.speaker: Wizard Traiborn
lines.text: Problem? There's no problem! Everything is fine here. We're having a party!

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I mean what's the problem they have to solve?

lines.speaker: Wizard Traiborn
lines.text: Oh! It's trivial! They just have to figure out what a thingummywut is!

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: A thingummy... what?

lines.speaker: Wizard Traiborn
lines.text: Precisely!

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Right... I'll speak to the apprentices. Perhaps together we can figure this out.

lines.speaker: Wizard Traiborn
lines.text: Good luck!

sections.title: Talking to the apprentices after speaking to Traiborn

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Could I see that puzzle again?

lines.speaker: Apprentice Cordelia
lines.text: Here it is.

lines.speaker: Apprentice Cordelia
lines.text: Thingummywut. Thin... gummy... what?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Traiborn said you're trying to solve a puzzle? Maybe I could help?

lines.speaker: Apprentice Cordelia
lines.text: Why? Do you think I need your help to solve this? I'll prove I'm smarter than these two!

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Oh, I don't doubt you're smart enough to solve this on your own. I'd just like to see the puzzle to further my own knowledge and understanding.

lines.speaker: Apprentice Cordelia
lines.text: You think I'll be persuaded that easily? I'm not as easy to fool as the others, but I admire your efforts nonetheless.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I'll be honest then. In truth, I don't care about this puzzle at all. I'm actually looking to recruit some talented wizards to help uncover a long-lost teleportation matrix.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: The hope is to use it to gain easy access to the runic altars for runecrafting purposes.

lines.speaker: Apprentice Cordelia
lines.text: Well that's infinitely more interesting! In that case, why do you care about the puzzle?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Traiborn said that he wouldn't give his permission for you to join me until the puzzle is solved, so I thought I'd lend a hand.

lines.speaker: Apprentice Cordelia
lines.text: Very well, you appear to be telling the truth, and I'd rather be uncovering a teleportation matrix than doing this.

lines.speaker: Apprentice Cordelia
lines.text: Here it is.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Could I see that puzzle again?

lines.speaker: Apprentice Felix
lines.text: Okay. Here you go!

lines.speaker: Apprentice Felix
lines.text: So, if that's six and this is eight, then if we multiply... No, divide? No, that's not it either.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Sounds like you're trying to solve a puzzle. Perhaps I could help?

lines.speaker: Apprentice Felix
lines.text: Oh, hey there! Yes, I'm trying to solve a puzzle set by the world-renowned Traiborn himself! It truly is an honour to have someone like him take the time to teach an up-and-coming wizard such as myself.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Why do you think so highly of him?

lines.speaker: Apprentice Felix
lines.text: Oh, so many reasons! But the biggest is that amongst all of the wizards, he is the foremost expert in extra- planar magic!

lines.speaker: Apprentice Felix
lines.text: Why else do you think he has the smallest room in the tower? Because he doesn't need the space! He can just open a portal to create more!

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I see, and is this the branch of magic you want to specialise in?

lines.speaker: Apprentice Felix
lines.text: Oh absolutely! That's why I'm here. I need to solve this puzzle to prove that I'm ready to learn all that he has to teach.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Well that's actually not too different to the reason I'm here. I'm currently helping a wizard called Persten, and she seems to be quite knowledgeable on different planes.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: She's tasked me with recruiting some wizards to help her uncover a teleportation matrix. Would you like to join us?

lines.speaker: Apprentice Felix
lines.text: You've met Persten? She studied directly under Traiborn for multiple years! I'd love to meet her, but she's hard to track down. Could you introduce us?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Sure, I can do that. We need to solve this puzzle first though. Traiborn won't let you three leave until you do. Perhaps I could help you solve it?

lines.speaker: Apprentice Felix
lines.text: I'm not sure. I wouldn't want Traiborn to think poorly of me if I've cheated and asked for help... but I do want to work with Persten...

lines.speaker: Apprentice Felix
lines.text: Okay, I'll show you the puzzle.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Could I see that puzzle again?

lines.speaker: Apprentice Tamara
lines.text: Sure! Just please don't tell the others you helped me.

lines.speaker: Apprentice Tamara
lines.text: Air and earth? What about water and fire? Is this an elemental problem, or are they completely irrelevant?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: What appears to be the problem?

lines.speaker: Apprentice Tamara
lines.text: Oh, sorry! Didn't see you there.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Don't worry. What were you muttering about with air and earth? Is it related to the puzzle Traiborn gave you all?

lines.speaker: Apprentice Tamara
lines.text: Yes, I just can't wrap my head around it. It feels like there are too many unknown variables. Besides, how does all this relate to a thingummywut in the first place? Whatever that actually is.

lines.speaker: Apprentice Tamara
lines.text: Perhaps it's a slayer creature? Or a person? Is this a logical puzzle, a riddle or a mathematical problem?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I see. Perhaps I could take a look and help you out?

lines.speaker: Apprentice Tamara
lines.text: I'm not sure... It sounds like cheating, but I really do want to beat Cordelia. She's always one step ahead of me, so it would be nice to finally win for a change.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Well, I'm actually here to recruit the three of you to help uncover an old teleportation matrix. The only issue is that Traiborn won't let you leave until the puzzle is solved.

lines.speaker: Apprentice Tamara
lines.text: Ooh! Did you say a teleportation matrix? Teleportation magic is my specialty! I'd love to help!

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Well, I'd be glad to have you along! So, will you show me the puzzle?

lines.speaker: Apprentice Tamara
lines.text: Sure! Just please don't tell the others you helped me.

sections.title: Talking to Traiborn after seeing all three riddles

lines.speaker: Wizard Traiborn
lines.text: Ello young thingummywut.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I think I know what a thingummywut is!

lines.speaker: Wizard Traiborn
lines.text: Oh? Do tell.

lines.speaker: Wizard Traiborn
lines.text: Well done! That's correct!

lines.speaker: Wizard Traiborn
lines.text: See, it's easier to solve problems when you put aside what's best for you and instead focus on what's best for the team!

lines.speaker: Wizard Traiborn
lines.text: Congratulations, you all pass!

lines.speaker: Apprentice Cordelia
lines.text: But you said that only the first of us to solve it would pass!

lines.speaker: Wizard Traiborn
lines.text: Oh? Did I? I forgot. Either way, I hope you've all now learnt a valuable lesson in teamwork! You have this adventurer here to thank for that.

lines.speaker: Apprentice Tamara
lines.text: Wait, but we all had different puzzles written down?

lines.speaker: Apprentice Felix
lines.text: Of course! We needed to combine our puzzles to solve it. Wow, that's genius! What a great way to teach us all teamwork!

lines.speaker: Wizard Traiborn
lines.text: Now, finish with a bang did you say?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: What the - what was that for?!

lines.speaker: Wizard Traiborn
lines.text: Well, it was your idea after all. Now why don't you go tell Sedridor that my apprentices are free to help?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: [number]!

lines.speaker: Wizard Traiborn
lines.text: No, that's not right at all. You should think harder about this!

lines.speaker: Wizard Traiborn
lines.text: Ello young thingummywut.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: So your apprentices can help with the incantation?

lines.speaker: Wizard Traiborn
lines.text: That's right, young thingummywut. Now, don't keep Sedridor waiting!

lines.speaker: Apprentice Cordelia
lines.text: Traiborn says I'm allowed to help you with this teleportation matrix. You should speak to Sedridor in the basement.

lines.speaker: Apprentice Felix
lines.text: I think I'm ready to meet Persten! You should go to the basement and let Sedridor know we're all ready.

lines.speaker: Apprentice Tamara
lines.text: Traiborn says I'm allowed to help you now. Let's find this teleportation matrix! I think you need to speak to Sedridor in the basement first though.

lines.speaker: Wizard Persten
lines.text: Hello again! Ready to uncover a magical secret that could change the world forever?

lines.speaker: Archmage Sedridor
lines.text: Slow down, Persten! I am still inspecting this amulet of yours.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: You're inspecting the amulet now? Does that mean the incantation is safe?

lines.speaker: Wizard Persten
lines.text: That's right! The amulet is as well, of course, but Sedridor here likes to be careful.

lines.speaker: Archmage Sedridor
lines.text: Not all of us are so carefree when it comes to the rules of our order, Persten. I mean really, Zamorakian incantations! Be glad I've even let you back in the tower!

lines.speaker: Wizard Persten
lines.text: The unfortunate price of progress, Sedridor.

lines.speaker: Archmage Sedridor
lines.text: I can think of a few other unfortunate things...

lines.speaker: Archmage Sedridor
lines.text: Anyway, the amulet seems to be safe, and as much as I hate to admit it, your assessment seems to be correct.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: So we can go ahead?

lines.speaker: Archmage Sedridor
lines.text: Yes. I'm still not happy about this, but we can't throw away this chance. I assume Traiborn's apprentices are ready?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: That's right.

lines.speaker: Wizard Persten
lines.text: Well then what are we waiting for? Let's do this!

lines.speaker: Archmage Sedridor
lines.text: [player name], are you ready?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Let's do it.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Not yet.

lines.speaker: Wizard Persten
lines.text: Don't take too long.

sections.title: After declining to start the incantation

lines.speaker: Archmage Sedridor
lines.text: Hello again, [player name]. What can I do for you?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: So we're ready to perform the incantation?

lines.speaker: Archmage Sedridor
lines.text: It seems so, assuming you are ready yourself?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Let's do it.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Not yet.

lines.speaker: Archmage Sedridor
lines.text: We will be here once you are.

lines.speaker: Wizard Persten
lines.text: Ready to begin the incantation?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Let's do it.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Not yet.

lines.speaker: Wizard Persten
lines.text: Don't take too long.

lines.speaker: Wizard Persten
lines.text: Everyone ready?

lines.speaker: Apprentice Felix
lines.text: We won't let you down, Persten.

lines.speaker: Wizard Persten
lines.text: Alright, with me!

lines.speaker: Persten and co.
lines.text: Saris! Val! Curak! Belito!

lines.speaker: Apprentice Tamara
lines.text: It worked!

lines.speaker: Apprentice Cordelia
lines.text: Well what are we waiting for? Let's go in!

lines.speaker: Archmage Sedridor
lines.text: Go in? You three aren't going through that portal. We have no idea what's on the other side!

lines.speaker: Archmage Sedridor
lines.text: I have given Persten and [player name] permission to go through. Until we know it's safe, no one else may enter.

lines.speaker: Apprentice Cordelia
lines.text: You can't be serious!

lines.speaker: Apprentice Tamara
lines.text: Please let us help! We've all worked so hard for an opportunity like this!

lines.speaker: Apprentice Felix
lines.text: And we have Persten to look after us!

lines.speaker: Archmage Sedridor
lines.text: No. You are only apprentices. You were here to help with the incantation, and you've done that now. You should go back to your studies.

lines.speaker: Wizard Traiborn
lines.text: Come now, Sedridor. The young thingummywuts have proven themselves to be more than capable. Let them have their party!

lines.speaker: Wizard Persten
lines.text: It's going to be quite something! You really should let them help!

lines.speaker: Archmage Sedridor
lines.text: Quiet, Persten!

lines.speaker: Archmage Sedridor
lines.text: Let it be known that I consider this a bad idea. That said, they are your apprentices, Traiborn. If they have your permission, I won't stop them.

lines.speaker: Wizard Persten
lines.text: Alright! Ready to make history?

lines.speaker: Apprentice Tamara
lines.text: Let's go!

sections.title: Talking to Archmage Sedridor after entering the portal

lines.speaker: Archmage Sedridor
lines.text: Hello again, [player name]. What can I do for you?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Are the others through the portal?

lines.speaker: Archmage Sedridor
lines.text: I assume so. You should probably go through and join them.

sections.title: Entering the portal before finishing the cutscene

lines.speaker: Wizard Persten
lines.text: Well this is... unexpected.

lines.speaker: Apprentice Cordelia
lines.text: Where are we?

lines.speaker: Apprentice Felix
lines.text: Are we... underwater?

lines.speaker: Apprentice Tamara
lines.text: Let me cast a locator spell!

lines.speaker: Apprentice Tamara
lines.text: It seems we're deep below the sea, just south of Morytania!

lines.speaker: Wizard Persten
lines.text: Fascinating...

lines.speaker: Wizard Persten
lines.text: Come, let's look around.

lines.speaker: Wizard Persten
lines.text: This place is brilliant! The apprentices are looking around to see what they can discover. You should join them!

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Any luck with this hole?

lines.speaker: Apprentice Cordelia
lines.text: Don't disturb me! I'll need absolute silence if I'm to work out what this mysterious energy is.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Found anything interesting?

lines.speaker: Apprentice Cordelia
lines.text: There's something strange about this big hole here. It's giving out the tiniest amount of magical energy. I can't work out what it is though.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: You know, those plants look similar to stuff I've seen in the...

lines.speaker: Apprentice Cordelia
lines.text: Quiet! I'm more than capable of working this out. Maybe you should go help the others instead.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Still looking at that statue?

lines.speaker: Apprentice Felix
lines.text: Isn't it brilliant? A statue of a justiciar! I wish I could have met her!

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: That's an interesting statue.

lines.speaker: Apprentice Felix
lines.text: See the plaque on it? It reads: 'Aliya, Justiciar of the Lion and guardian of the Temple of the Eye.'

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Temple of the Eye?

lines.speaker: Apprentice Felix
lines.text: That must be where we are right now! I've read about the justiciars before, in a book back at the Wizards' Tower.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: What did the book say?

lines.speaker: Apprentice Felix
lines.text: They were powerful warriors in service to Saradomin himself, back when the gods still walked among us! This temple must be ancient. Imagine if Saradomin himself once stood here!

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Discovered anything more about these rune guardians?

lines.speaker: Apprentice Tamara
lines.text: Not yet, but their mere presence here is an amazing discovery!

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: What are you looking at?

lines.speaker: Apprentice Tamara
lines.text: Look! Rune guardians, like the ones at the Mage Training Arena! If this place was once a teleportation matrix, these rune guardians might have helped the wizards that used it.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Anything different about them?

lines.speaker: Apprentice Tamara
lines.text: Well, despite their age, they're actually of a remarkably similar design to the ones used by modern wizards. Isn't that amazing?

sections.title: Talking to Wizard Persten after speaking with the apprentices

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Hey!

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Hello?

lines.speaker: Wizard Persten
lines.text: Oh, sorry, I didn't see you there. I was lost in thought!

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: What were you thinking about?

lines.speaker: Wizard Persten
lines.text: Hmm? Oh, I was looking at these markings here. They're very interesting! They seem to be radiating a magical energy I've never encountered before!

lines.speaker: Wizard Persten
lines.text: Take a closer look! Maybe you'll feel the energy as well!

lines.speaker: First Mysterious Voice
lines.text: Saradomin be with you.

lines.speaker: Second Mysterious Voice
lines.text: And with you, Zachariah. So, it's really true?

lines.speaker: Zachariah
lines.text: Indeed it is, Aliya, and he has given us the task of protecting it.

lines.speaker: Aliya
lines.text: What would he have me do?

lines.speaker: Zachariah
lines.text: You must build a sanctuary for the Eye. The forces of chaos already seek to reobtain it, as do countless others.

lines.speaker: Aliya
lines.text: I will keep it safe, no matter the cost. And you?

lines.speaker: Zachariah
lines.text: Zamorak has tasked his collector with finding the Eye. I will hunt him down before he does, along with any others who would try to claim it.

lines.speaker: Aliya
lines.text: Then I wish you luck, brother.

lines.speaker: Zachariah
lines.text: There is one last thing, Aliya. You must keep the Eye secret from all others, even our own. The fewer people that know the truth, the better.

lines.speaker: Aliya
lines.text: On my honour as a justiciar, it will be done.

lines.speaker: Zachariah
lines.text: Good. Saradomin be with you, sister.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: What the...

lines.speaker: Wizard Persten
lines.text: [player name]?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Jas!

lines.speaker: Wizard Persten
lines.text: What did you say?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I... I don't know.

lines.speaker: Wizard Persten
lines.text: Did you just say... 'Jas'?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I can't remember. When I looked at the marks on the floor I had this... vision, but it's all unclear.

lines.speaker: Wizard Persten
lines.text: Curious...

lines.speaker: Wizard Persten
lines.text: Well, time to see what the others have found!

sections.title: Talking to Wizard Persten after starting the Abyssal rift cutscene

lines.speaker: Wizard Persten
lines.text: Hey! Let's see what the others have found!

lines.speaker: Wizard Persten
lines.text: Right, what have we discovered?

lines.speaker: Apprentice Cordelia
lines.text: That large hole behind you is radiating a strange energy. I'm still trying to work out what it is.

lines.speaker: Wizard Persten
lines.text: Need a hand with that?

lines.speaker: Apprentice Cordelia
lines.text: No, I can work it out.

lines.speaker: Wizard Persten
lines.text: You sure can! What else do we have?

lines.speaker: Apprentice Tamara
lines.text: There's some old rune guardians here with eye symbols on them.

lines.speaker: Wizard Persten
lines.text: So this place was definitely used by wizards at one point. Amazing!

lines.speaker: Apprentice Felix
lines.text: Not just any wizards, Saradominist wizards! There's some statues of an old Saradominist Justiciar called Aliya!

lines.speaker: Wizard Persten
lines.text: Remarkable! The justiciars haven't been around for thousands of years. That confirms my suspicions about the age of this place.

lines.speaker: Apprentice Felix
lines.text: That's not all I found. Apparently, this place was once called the Temple of the Eye!

lines.speaker: Apprentice Cordelia
lines.text: The Eye? What's the Eye?

lines.speaker: Apprentice Tamara
lines.text: I remember reading about something called the Eye back at the Wizards' Tower! I only came across mention of it once, but apparently it was a powerful artefact made by Saradomin himself!

lines.speaker: Wizard Persten
lines.text: Sorry... made by Saradomin, you say?

lines.speaker: Apprentice Tamara
lines.text: That's what the book said. Why?

lines.speaker: Wizard Persten
lines.text: Oh, just checking! I get all caught up in the moment and mishear things sometimes.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: This 'Eye'... did the book say what it looked like?

lines.speaker: Apprentice Tamara
lines.text: It didn't. It didn't actually have any other information on it. I tried to learn more from other books, but found nothing.

lines.speaker: Apprentice Felix
lines.text: Anything else was probably lost when the first Wizards' Tower burnt down.

lines.speaker: Apprentice Cordelia
lines.text: Of course! I've worked out what the energy from that hole is. It's abyssal energy!

lines.speaker: Wizard Persten
lines.text: Hmm... Yes... Brilliant!

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Persten?

lines.speaker: Wizard Persten
lines.text: Ah, sorry! Lost in thought again! It was for a good reason though. I think I've just about worked it all out!

lines.speaker: Apprentice Felix
lines.text: Amazing as always, Persten! We're so lucky to be able to work with you.

lines.speaker: Wizard Persten
lines.text: Thank you! So, I reckon my theory that this is an old teleportation matrix was spot on! The wizards here must have used power from the Abyss to create portals to the runic altars.

lines.speaker: Wizard Persten
lines.text: Then, with the power from this 'Eye' and support from their rune guardians, they'd have been able to create runes at lightning speed!

lines.speaker: Wizard Persten
lines.text: The Eye must have had a huge impact on their work. They named the temple after it and fashioned a symbol based on it.

lines.speaker: Apprentice Tamara
lines.text: And we're still using that symbol today! We just never knew why until now!

lines.speaker: Apprentice Felix
lines.text: Should we report back to Sedridor?

lines.speaker: Wizard Persten
lines.text: In a moment. I'm just going to cast a simple revealing spell first, just in case there's anything else hidden here.

lines.speaker: Apprentice Tamara
lines.text: What's happening?

lines.speaker: Wizard Persten
lines.text: Don't worry! I'm sure everything is fine. Let me handle this.

lines.speaker: Wizard Persten
lines.text: Huh... now that is a problem.

lines.speaker: Apprentice Felix
lines.text: Persten!

lines.speaker: Apprentice Cordelia
lines.text: You don't belong in this temple, accursed filth!

lines.speaker: The Great Guardian
lines.text: And so the rift to the Scar is open once more. Humans, fight with us. Let us hold back the Abyss!

sections.title: Talking to Sedridor after the portal is open

lines.speaker: Archmage Sedridor
lines.text: Hello again, [player name]. What can I do for you?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Sedridor! Something's gone wrong!

lines.speaker: Archmage Sedridor
lines.text: What do you mean?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Through the portal! We've opened some sort of rift to the Abyss!

lines.speaker: Archmage Sedridor
lines.text: Well you'd better get back there and fix it!

sections.title: Talking to Apprentice Felix after the Great Guardian spawns

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Are you okay?

lines.speaker: Apprentice Felix
lines.text: I... I think so. Sorry... I need a moment to collect myself.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I'll go and check on Tamara and Cordelia. I'll be back soon

sections.title: Talking to Tamara or Cordelia after the Great Guardian spawns, or reenterring the portal

lines.speaker: Apprentice Tamara
lines.text: What do we do now? What if more of those creatures come through that rift?

lines.speaker: Apprentice Cordelia
lines.text: Well don't think I'll be stopping them all again! I may be powerful, but that spell still took a lot out of me.

lines.speaker: The Great Guardian
lines.text: Humans, use the cells. Protect me while I close the rift.

lines.speaker: Apprentice Tamara
lines.text: Cells?

lines.speaker: Apprentice Felix
lines.text: Tamara! [player name]! I think I've found something over here!

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: On my way!

lines.speaker: Apprentice Cordelia
lines.text: If you're hoping I can help in some way, you're out of luck. I may be powerful, but that spell still took a lot out of me. You'll have to hope the others can do something.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I'll go and see what Felix has found.

lines.speaker: Apprentice Tamara
lines.text: Okay.

lines.speaker: Apprentice Felix
lines.text: You should take one of these containers to Tamara. Just don't take more than one or they'll explode!

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Alright, thanks for the warning.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: What have you found?

lines.speaker: Apprentice Felix
lines.text: Well... I can't say for sure, but I think the containers on this table contain small amounts of runic energy.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Right, I'll take one to Tamara.

lines.speaker: Apprentice Felix
lines.text: Just make sure you don't take more than one! I tried to do that already and the energy within them exploded!

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Alright, thanks for the warning.

lines.speaker: Apprentice Tamara
lines.text: Try placing that cell in the tile next to me.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Alright.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: This is what Felix found.

lines.speaker: Apprentice Tamara
lines.text: That must be one of the cells we need! Try placing it in the tile next to me.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Alright.

sections.title: After placing the weak cell in the inactive cell tile

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I'll see if Felix can help work out what to do next.

lines.speaker: Apprentice Tamara
lines.text: Be careful!

lines.speaker: Apprentice Tamara
lines.text: It worked! This barrier should protect us against those creatures!

lines.speaker: Apprentice Tamara
lines.text: And just in time too. Here come more of them!

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: What do we do now?

lines.speaker: Apprentice Tamara
lines.text: I'm not sure. The barrier won't hold out for long!

lines.speaker: Great Guardian
lines.text: Humans, we need more support. Restore my brethren! Place cells within imbued rocks and they will do the rest.

lines.speaker: Apprentice Tamara
lines.text: Imbued rocks? What does that mean?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I'll see if Felix can help.

lines.speaker: Apprentice Tamara
lines.text: Be careful!

sections.title: Talking to Felix after placing the weak cell

lines.speaker: Apprentice Felix
lines.text: Try infusing each of the essence piles here with a cell. You'll probably need a chisel to shape the essence first. Looks like there's one on the floor there.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Right, I'll get to work.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Felix, we need to place cells in some imbued rocks. Any idea what that means?

lines.speaker: Apprentice Felix
lines.text: I think the essence piles here are actually remains of old guardians. They seem to contain elemental and catalytic energies. Perhaps you could try infusing each one with a cell.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Good idea.

lines.speaker: Apprentice Felix
lines.text: You'll probably need a chisel to shape the essence first. Looks like there's one on the floor there.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Right, I'll get to work.

sections.title: After assembling the first essence pile

lines.speaker: Apprentice Felix
lines.text: Try infusing the other essence pile with a cell like you did with the first one.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Will do.

lines.speaker: Apprentice Felix
lines.text: It worked! Looks like it's following the elemental symbols on the ground.

lines.speaker: Apprentice Felix
lines.text: Try creating another one using the other essence pile.

sections.title: After assembling the second essence pile

lines.speaker: Apprentice Felix
lines.text: The guardians should be strong enough to fend off those creatures. Maybe Tamara will know what to do next.

lines.speaker: Apprentice Felix
lines.text: Amazing! Looks like this one's following the catalytic symbols.

lines.speaker: Apprentice Felix
lines.text: These guardians should be strong enough to fend off those creatures. Head back to Tamara and see what we should do next.

sections.title: Talking to Tamara after assembling both essence piles

lines.speaker: Apprentice Tamara
lines.text: Do you have anything to capture some runic energy with?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Not yet.

lines.speaker: Apprentice Tamara
lines.text: Try mining some fragments from the fallen guardians. Once you have a few, go and speak to Felix. I'm sure he'll be able to help you use them.

lines.speaker: Apprentice Tamara
lines.text: You made some rune guardians!

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: And they seem to be holding back those creatures.

lines.speaker: The Great Guardian
lines.text: Humans, I lack the power needed to close the rift! I require runic energy!

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Runic energy? Do we need more cells?

lines.speaker: Apprentice Tamara
lines.text: Hmm... I don't think they'll work for this. We'll need something else...

lines.speaker: Apprentice Tamara
lines.text: Of course! We can use the rune guardians! See all the fallen guardians around here? They'd have once been powered using runic energy!

lines.speaker: Apprentice Tamara
lines.text: If you were to obtain some fragments from them, you might be able to make something to capture the runic energy needed.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: So mine the fallen guardians?

lines.speaker: Apprentice Tamara
lines.text: Exactly! Hopefully you can find a pickaxe somewhere. Once you have a few fragments, go and speak to Felix. I'm sure he'll be able to help you use them.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I think I have enough fragments. I should speak to Felix about what to do with them.

sections.title: Talking to Felix after getting guardian fragments

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: We need to power up this big guardian somehow, but I need a way to capture runic energy. Tamara said you might be able to help.

lines.speaker: Apprentice Felix
lines.text: Hmm... I reckon this old workbench here might have been used for just that. There's still plenty of pure essence on it for you to use, but that might not be enough...

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I have some fragments from the fallen guardians. What if I combined the pure essence with them?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: What if I combined the pure essence with fragments from the fallen guardians?

lines.speaker: Apprentice Felix
lines.text: Yes! That would be perfect!

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I'll give it a go.

sections.title: Talking to Tamara after getting guardian fragments

lines.speaker: Apprentice Tamara
lines.text: Do you have anything to capture some runic energy with?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I have some fragments from the fallen guardians.

lines.speaker: Apprentice Tamara
lines.text: Take them to Felix. He'll hopefully know what to do with them.

sections.title: Talking to Felix after making guardian essence

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I used the old workbench to make some essence!

lines.speaker: Apprentice Felix
lines.text: If you think you have enough, you should take it to Tamara.

sections.title: Talking to Tamara after making guardian essence

lines.speaker: Apprentice Tamara
lines.text: Head to the Mind Altar and craft some runes. That essence you made should capture some of the runic energy released. Take an uncharged cell with you as well.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Why?

lines.speaker: Apprentice Tamara
lines.text: I have a theory to test.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Fair enough. I'll be back soon.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Do you think this will work?

lines.speaker: Apprentice Tamara
lines.text: Perfect! Now we just need to capture some runic energy with it.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: How?

lines.speaker: The Great Guardian
lines.text: I shall now use power from the Abyss to open a portal to the Runic Altar of Mind.

lines.speaker: Apprentice Tamara
lines.text: Well... I guess that's your answer. Head to the Mind Altar and craft some runes. That essence you have should capture some of the runic energy released. Take an uncharged cell with you as well.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Why?

lines.speaker: Apprentice Tamara
lines.text: I have a theory to test.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Fair enough. I'll be back soon.

sections.title: Talking to Felix after Tamara asks to go to the Mind Altar

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: What should I do now?

lines.speaker: Apprentice Felix
lines.text: I'm not sure. Maybe Tamara will know what to do.

sections.title: Talking to Tamara after returning from the Mind Altar

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: What do we do now?

lines.speaker: Apprentice Tamara
lines.text: First, you should use that new cell to replace the one powering the barrier here. I don't think the current one will last much longer.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: And then?

lines.speaker: Apprentice Tamara
lines.text: Then just use those stones to power up this big guardian.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I'll get right on it.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Look! This is what happened when I took that essence to the Mind Altar.

lines.speaker: Apprentice Tamara
lines.text: Great! That's exactly what I was hoping for. What about the cell?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: It seemed to capture energy from the altar as well.

lines.speaker: Apprentice Tamara
lines.text: I had a feeling it would! It's all coming together!

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: What do we do now?

lines.speaker: Apprentice Tamara
lines.text: First, you should use that new cell to replace the one powering the barrier here. I don't think the current one will last much longer.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: And then?

lines.speaker: Apprentice Tamara
lines.text: Then just use those stones to power up this big guardian.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I'll get right on it.

sections.title: Talking to Tamara after replacing the weak cell

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: What do we do now?

lines.speaker: Apprentice Tamara
lines.text: Just use those stones to power up this big guardian.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I'll get right on it.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: What do we do now?

lines.speaker: Apprentice Tamara
lines.text: Create some more of that essence and use it to capture energy from the Water Altar. You should then be able to use it to power up the guardian again. Make sure you take another uncharged cell with you.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Alright, I'll be back.

lines.speaker: The Great Guardian
lines.text: Human, the stones have fueled me, but more are needed.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: The guardian still needs more energy!

lines.speaker: Apprentice Tamara
lines.text: I guess we need to repeat the process again.

lines.speaker: The Great Guardian
lines.text: I shall now use power from the Abyss to open a portal to the Runic Altar of Water.

lines.speaker: Apprentice Tamara
lines.text: Looks like the portal to the Mind Altar has closed. I guess the guardian only keeps them open for so long. You'll need to use the new portal instead. Make sure you take another uncharged cell with you.

sections.title: Talking to Felix before powering the guardian

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: What should I do to power up the big guardian?

lines.speaker: Apprentice Felix
lines.text: You could use the workbench here to combine pure essence with fragments from the nearby fallen guardians. The essence would then be perfect for capturing runic energy.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I'll give it a go.

sections.title: Talking to Tamara again after getting essence

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: What do we do now?

lines.speaker: Apprentice Tamara
lines.text: Use some more of that essence to capture energy from the Water Altar. You should then be able to use it to power up the guardian again. Make sure you take another uncharged cell with you.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Alright, I'll be back.

sections.title: Talking to Felix after returning from the Water Altar

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I have some stones I can use to power the big guardian.

lines.speaker: Apprentice Felix
lines.text: Great! You should go do that now.

sections.title: Talking to Tamara after returning from the Water Altar

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: What do we do now?

lines.speaker: Apprentice Tamara
lines.text: Just use those stones to power up the guardian again.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Alright, I'll get right on it.

sections.title: Powering the guardian the second time

lines.speaker: The Great Guardian
lines.text: Human, the stones have fueled me, but more are needed.

sections.title: Talking to Tamara after powering the Great Guardian

lines.speaker: Apprentice Tamara
lines.text: Try making another rune guardian using that new cell. In the meantime, I'll see if I can work out what this talisman does.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Alright.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Something odd happened at the Water Altar. The energy in this cell seems different to before. The essence also formed this strange talisman.

lines.speaker: Apprentice Tamara
lines.text: Oh, fascinating! I guess it makes sense that the stronger the runic energy, the more powerful the cell will be. After all, water is more powerful than air or mind.

lines.speaker: Apprentice Tamara
lines.text: Try making another rune guardian using that cell. In the meantime, I'll see if I can work out what this talisman does.

sections.title: Talking to Felix before making another guardian

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I need to make another rune guardian.

lines.speaker: Apprentice Felix
lines.text: Go right ahead. Just use a chisel to shape one of these essence piles and then infuse the result with a cell.

lines.speaker: Apprentice Felix
lines.text: Look at that! I don't know what you did, but that guardian is much stronger than the ones you made before.

sections.title: Talking to Tamara after constructing the medium elemental guardian

lines.speaker: Apprentice Tamara
lines.text: Just present that talisman to the statue where the portal to the Water Altar was. Make sure you take more essence and another cell with you.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: That cell made a more powerful rune guardian!

lines.speaker: Apprentice Tamara
lines.text: Great! We're almost there! I think we just need to power up this guardian one more time for it to be able to close that rift.

lines.speaker: The Great Guardian
lines.text: I must conserve my power. The portal to the Runic Altar of Water has closed.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: But we need that portal!

lines.speaker: Apprentice Tamara
lines.text: Don't worry, I think there's another way. I reckon that talisman you gave me can be used to return to the Water Altar without the portal being open.

lines.speaker: Apprentice Tamara
lines.text: Just present that talisman to the statue where the portal to the Water Altar was. Make sure you take more essence and another cell with you.

sections.title: Talking to Tamara again after returning from the Water Altar

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I have another cell. Do you want me to power up the barrier again?

lines.speaker: Apprentice Tamara
lines.text: That's right! If my theory is correct, it should produce an even stronger barrier!

lines.speaker: Apprentice Tamara
lines.text: Let's charge the guardian with those stones you created and close this rift!

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Look! The barrier changed!

lines.speaker: Apprentice Tamara
lines.text: Just as I thought! We can create better barriers by using stronger cells!

lines.speaker: Apprentice Tamara
lines.text: Now, let's charge the guardian with those stones you created and close this rift!

lines.speaker: The Great Guardian
lines.text: Humans, I now have the power to close the rift. Brace yourselves!

lines.speaker: The Great Guardian
lines.text: We are safe, for now.

lines.speaker: Apprentice Tamara
lines.text: What was all that? What happened?

lines.speaker: The Great Guardian
lines.text: That was a rift into an area of the Abyss known as the Scar.

lines.speaker: Apprentice Cordelia
lines.text: The Scar? But I thought this was meant to be a teleportation matrix.

lines.speaker: The Great Guardian
lines.text: You are mistaken. While it is true that teleportation magic is strong here, that is only a side effect of this temple's history.

lines.speaker: Apprentice Felix
lines.text: What history?

lines.speaker: The Great Guardian
lines.text: This temple was built to house a powerful artefact. Many sought to claim this artefact for themselves, including servants of the Fallen Empire.

lines.speaker: The Great Guardian
lines.text: In their attempt to claim the artefact, they used magic they did not understand. The rift to the Scar was created as a result.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: This artefact... you mean the Eye?

lines.speaker: The Great Guardian
lines.text: You are correct. The Eye was removed after the battle, but not before the Justiciar of the Lion used it to seal the rift once more.

lines.speaker: The Great Guardian
lines.text: She then created us, the Guardians of the Rift, to protect this place in the event the rift was ever reopened.

lines.speaker: Apprentice Felix
lines.text: And we reopened it.

lines.speaker: Apprentice Cordelia
lines.text: But it's been sealed again now! The danger is gone.

lines.speaker: The Great Guardian
lines.text: Incorrect. Without the power of the Eye, we have no way to seal the rift permanently. It will soon reopen once more. You should leave before that happens.

lines.speaker: Apprentice Tamara
lines.text: No. This was our mistake. I'm staying to help fix it.

lines.speaker: Apprentice Felix
lines.text: Me too!

lines.speaker: Apprentice Cordelia
lines.text: Well if you two are staying, I guess I will as well.

lines.speaker: The Great Guardian
lines.text: Your support will be invaluable.

lines.speaker: Apprentice Tamara
lines.text: [player name], could you go back to the Wizards' Tower and let Sedridor know what happened here?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Alright, if you're absolutely sure about this.

lines.speaker: Apprentice Felix
lines.text: What about Persten? We have to help her.

lines.speaker: The Great Guardian
lines.text: No part of the Abyss is more dangerous than the Scar. Your friend will have already been consumed.

lines.speaker: The Great Guardian
lines.text: Now, the power of the Abyss grows. The rift will soon reopen once more. Those of you staying should prepare yourselves.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Then I guess I'd better go and see Sedridor. Good luck.

sections.title: Talking to Sedridor after leaving the temple, or using the portal

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Sedridor! It all went wrong!

lines.speaker: Archmage Sedridor
lines.text: What do you mean? Was there no teleportation matrix?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Kind of, but not really. The portal took us to the Temple of the Eye, an ancient Saradominist temple built to house a powerful artefact.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Long ago, a rift to the Abyss was created in the temple, but it was sealed. At least it was, until Persten accidently opened it again...

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: She was sucked into the Abyss, and all these abyssal creatures came out!

lines.speaker: Archmage Sedridor
lines.text: Abyssal creatures? We have to get in there and stop this!

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: It's okay again, for now. Some rune guardians called the Guardians of the Rift were built to protect the temple from the Abyss. They helped us close the rift again, but they say it will soon reopen.

lines.speaker: Archmage Sedridor
lines.text: This is a disaster! I knew that teleportation matrix was too good to be true. I should have stopped this, but I was blinded by my own desires for that matrix!

lines.speaker: Archmage Sedridor
lines.text: What of Traiborn's apprentices?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: They're safe. They chose to stay and help the Guardians of the Rift keep the rift closed.

lines.speaker: Archmage Sedridor
lines.text: Then I will ensure they have all the help they need. I will also begin researching this rift at once. Maybe we can still find a way to close it permanently.

lines.speaker: Archmage Sedridor
lines.text: In the meantime, I suggest you do what you can to help them as well. After all, this is partially your doing.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I know. I'll help wherever I'm needed.

lines.speaker: Archmage Sedridor
lines.text: Thank you, [player name]. I appreciate that you did not mean for things to go this way. You should have this. It belonged to Persten. I don't want to think about where she got it.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Do you think she's still alive?

lines.speaker: Archmage Sedridor
lines.text: Sadly, that seems unlikely. Still, if anyone can survive the Abyss, it's her.

lines.speaker: Archmage Sedridor
lines.text: Now, we'd better get to work.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Hello there.

lines.speaker: Archmage Sedridor
lines.text: Hello again, [player name]. What can I do for you?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: How are things at the Temple of the Eye?

lines.speaker: Archmage Sedridor
lines.text: Between the Guardians of the Rift and ourselves, we are managing to keep the Abyss at bay.

lines.speaker: Archmage Sedridor
lines.text: However, despite all our research, we are no closer to working out how to close the rift permanently.

lines.speaker: Archmage Sedridor
lines.text: Gaining a better understanding of the artefact that was once there is key, but we have found little mention of this 'Eye'.

lines.speaker: Archmage Sedridor
lines.text: I'm sure the wizards of the first tower had plenty of research on it. After all, they knew enough about it to use its symbol as the symbol for our order.

lines.speaker: Archmage Sedridor
lines.text: All that knowledge lost because of those accursed Zamorakians!

lines.speaker: Archmage Sedridor
lines.text: Anyway, if you wish to help defend the temple, just head through the portal.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Could you tell me about the old Wizards' Tower?

lines.speaker: Archmage Sedridor
lines.text: Of course. The first Wizards' Tower was built at the same time the Order of Wizards was founded. It was at the dawn of the Fifth Age, when the secrets of runecrafting were rediscovered.

lines.speaker: Archmage Sedridor
lines.text: For years, the tower was a hub of magical research. Wizards of all races and religions were welcomed into our order.

lines.speaker: Archmage Sedridor
lines.text: Alas, that openness is what ultimately led to disaster. The wizards who served Zamorak, the evil god of chaos, tried to claim our magical discoveries in his name.

lines.speaker: Archmage Sedridor
lines.text: They failed, but in retaliation, they burnt the entire tower to the ground. Years of work was destroyed.

lines.speaker: Archmage Sedridor
lines.text: The tower was soon rebuilt of course, but even now we are still trying to regain knowledge that was lost.

lines.speaker: Archmage Sedridor
lines.text: That's why I spend my time down here, in fact. This basement is all that is left of the old tower, and I believe there are still some secrets to discover here.

lines.speaker: Archmage Sedridor
lines.text: Of course, one secret I am no longer looking for is the teleportation incantation to the Rune Essence Mine. We have you to thank for that.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I've heard a different take on how the tower burnt down. Something about a ritual gone wrong?

lines.speaker: Archmage Sedridor
lines.text: Nothing more than Zamorakian hearsay! They burnt the tower down and are now too cowardly to even admit it!

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Fair enough.

lines.speaker: Mage of Zamorak
lines.text: Ah, you again. What do you want?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: About that incantation your initiate gave me...

lines.speaker: Mage of Zamorak
lines.text: Ah yes, the one to trace that amulet. Don't think I didn't notice that you modified the transmission spell I put on it. Next time you need help, we might not be so willing.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Well maybe you should have asked first! Besides, you wouldn't have got anything useful out of it. In tracing that amulet, we accidently managed to open an uncontrollable rift to the Abyss.

lines.speaker: Mage of Zamorak
lines.text: And does this 'we' include the Order of Wizards?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Maybe...

lines.speaker: Mage of Zamorak
lines.text: I knew it! It's just like them to play with magic they don't understand. First they burnt down the Wizards' Tower, now they're opening uncontrollable abyssal rifts!

lines.speaker: Mage of Zamorak
lines.text: Perhaps the fools should leave the dangerous magic to us next time!

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Oh, that reminds me. That incantation didn't appear to be written on parchment. What was it?

lines.speaker: Mage of Zamorak
lines.text: Hmm... probably the flesh of an abyssal creature. Why do you ask?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Oh, I just thought it might be human flesh. I'm relieved it wasn't!

lines.speaker: Mage of Zamorak
lines.text: You've been spending too much time with your friends in the Order of Wizards! I know that we Zamorakians have a bad reputation, but do you really think we'd be so barbaric?
